description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Closes **F8 / CF5** (HIGH consensus from Idioms + Distributed + Performance — Idioms H1, Distributed MEDIUM-3, Performance F-PERF-09).

Two helpers (`safe_compile_quoted` in mutation_runner.ex:402-436 and `capture_stderr` in mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex:186-207) use a process-dictionary side-channel to extract a value from the closure passed into `ExUnit.CaptureIO.capture_io/2`. Works only because `capture_io` happens to execute the closure synchronously in the calling process — undocumented implementation detail.

The stdlib already provides `ExUnit.CaptureIO.with_io/3` which returns `{result, captured_string}` directly. Replacing the pattern removes ~30 LOC per call site, eliminates the `make_ref` / `Process.put` / `Process.delete` lifetime bookkeeping, and removes the brittle dependency on capture_io implementation details.

## Deliverable

- Replace both call sites with `ExUnit.CaptureIO.with_io/3` (note: requires Elixir 1.10+; project is on 1.19.5).
- Delete `make_ref` / `Process.put` / `Process.delete` scaffolding at both sites.
- Update any tests that depended on the smuggle pattern (probably none — tests should be black-box).
- Revise `.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md`: capture-io contract updates if any (this is largely an internal refactor; spec may not need touching).

## Verification

- Existing tests in mutation_runner_test.exs continue to pass without modification (behavior-equivalent)
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors / mix test green
- LOC delta: ~-60 lines (~30 per site)

## Allowed touches

- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex (lines 402-436 region)
- lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex (lines 186-207 region)
- Corresponding test files only if behaviorally needed
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md only if a spec invariant becomes documentable
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope

- Do not refactor `MutationLoop.run/1` orchestration beyond the capture-io swap — that is .18 + .25 territory
- Do not change which streams are captured (still stderr in the loop, mixed for the compile)

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix test green (no test changes expected)
- [ ] LOC delta is negative (verify with git diff --stat)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

Verification stubs flipped: none (all stubs covering the affected requirements — v2 for r9, v5 for r12 — were already `execute: true`; no flipping needed)

Summary: Replaced the `ExUnit.CaptureIO.capture_io/2` + `make_ref`/`Process.put`/`Process.get`/`Process.delete` smuggle pattern at both call sites (`MutationRunner.safe_compile_quoted/3` and `MutationLoop.capture_stderr/2`) with `ExUnit.CaptureIO.with_io/3`, which returns `{closure_result, captured_io}` directly. The seam contract for the `:capture_io` config key now requires `with_io/3` instead of `capture_io/2`; `CaptureIoStub` (delegate to the real ExUnit.CaptureIO) and `RaisingCaptureIO` (call-counting fault injector — still fires on call #2, the inside-window capture) were updated in lock-step. Spec `mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r9` was updated to name `with_io/3`; the s9 example phrase referencing "MutationLoop.run/1's internal smuggle pattern failing" was reworded (the pattern no longer exists). `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean, `mix test` green (360/360, 15 excluded), `mix test --only spike` green (2/2, 373 excluded). LOC delta on production+test code: -13 (28 ins, 41 del); total commit -2 (46 ins, 46 del) once spec wording and CHANGELOG entry are included.

## Scope check (mechanical)
Files touched (6):
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex                  -- Allowed (lines 663-697, region matches 402-436 in pre-.32 numbering for safe_compile_quoted/3)
  - lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex    -- Allowed (capture_stderr/2 at line 325, region matches 186-207 pre-.32)
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs           -- Allowed (CaptureIoStub seam — "if behaviorally needed")
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs     -- Allowed (RaisingCaptureIO seam — same justification)
  - .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md              -- Allowed (r9 wording + s9 prose reflecting refactor)
  - CHANGELOG.md                                       -- Allowed
No Out-of-Scope files touched. Both lib hunks are single-hunk, region-local to the two named helpers; MutationLoop.run/1 orchestration untouched. Streams captured remain `:stderr` at both sites (no change in capture surface).

## Verification stubs
- v2  mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs       -- 22/22 pass
- v5  mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_raise_test.exs -- 7/7 pass
- Project verify: mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test 360/360 (15 excluded)
v1/v3/v4/v6 not affected by this refactor (no surface change there) and not re-run.

## r9 must-requirement trace (relevant must for this ticket)
- r9 spec wording: now reads `ExUnit.CaptureIO.with_io(:stderr, ...)` (confirmed at .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md:148). Spec ↔ code aligned.
- Test: `r9: stderr captured into results[i].warnings (s8)` at test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs:952-974. Stubs the compiler to emit `IO.write(:stderr, "warning: this is a captured stderr line\n")` and asserts the line lands in `result.warnings`.
- Concrete failure modes the test catches:
  (a) `with_io/3` swap dropping the captured-string tuple element -> `result.warnings` empty -> `Enum.any?(...)` false -> test fails.
  (b) Stderr suppression broken -> warning text written to actual stderr; either ExUnit complains via captured-IO assertions in surrounding harness or `result.warnings` is missing the line.

## SOURCE-CLAIM trace
1. safe_compile_quoted/3 swapped to with_io/3: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:672 — `apply(capture_io, :with_io, [:stderr, fn -> ... end])`. The closure now returns `{:ok, modules}` / `{:error, msg}` directly; outer `case body_result` consumes the tuple's first element. `make_ref` / `Process.put({__MODULE__, ref}, ...)` / `Process.get({__MODULE__, ref})` / `Process.delete({__MODULE__, ref})` all removed (verified via grep — no residual `{__MODULE__, ref}` keys remain anywhere in lib/).
2. capture_stderr/2 swapped to with_io/3: CONFIRMED. lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex:333 — `{body_result, output} = apply(capture_io, :with_io, [:stderr, fun])`. Single-line replacement; `make_ref` / `Process.put` / `Process.get` / `Process.delete` scaffolding all deleted.
3. Test stubs updated in lock-step: CONFIRMED.
   - CaptureIoStub (mutation_runner_test.exs:228-236) now `defdelegate with_io(device, fun), to: ExUnit.CaptureIO` — delegates to real impl, returns `{result, captured}`.
   - RaisingCaptureIO (mutation_runner_raise_test.exs:59-86) renames `capture_io/2` -> `with_io/2`; call-count semantics preserved (still increments `:raising_capture_io_call_n`, still defaults `fire_on=2`, still 3 invocations per site = mutated compile / capture_stderr / restore compile); fault path correctly raises before yielding the tuple so the exception propagates exactly as before; non-fault path now returns `{result, output}` instead of bare `output`.
4. LOC delta net negative: CONFIRMED. `git diff --shortstat lib/ test/` = 4 files changed, 28 insertions, 41 deletions (-13 lines). Full commit (incl. spec + CHANGELOG): 46/46 (net 0; the negative production+test delta is dwarfed by CHANGELOG entry growth, which is fine).

## Out-of-Scope (intent) check
- MutationLoop.run/1 orchestration: untouched. Only `capture_stderr/2` private helper inside the module was modified. No changes to timeout path, supervisor wiring, snapshot logic, or shutdown sequencing.
- Streams captured: both sites still `:stderr`. No change.
No .18 / .25 territory creep.

## V2 stub modification audit
The implementer modified test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs (the v2 stub):
- Only change is CaptureIoStub flipping `defdelegate capture_io(...)` -> `defdelegate with_io(...)`. This is a seam adaptation forced by the production-code contract change, not an assertion-level edit. None of the r3/r4/r5/r6/r7/r8/r9 test bodies were touched; the r9 test (lines 952-974) was inspected and is byte-identical to its pre-.23 form aside from indentation invariants. No prior assertion weakened or removed.

## Notes (non-blocking, not failures)
- mutation_loop.ex:38 has a stale moduledoc comment still naming `ExUnit.CaptureIO.capture_io(:stderr, fn -> ...)`. Cosmetic only (comment in a `# 2. **Stderr capture.**` overview block); not a functional issue, but worth a sweep on a future docs touch.

## Audit-expanded scope
n/a (iteration 1; no prior bounces)

## Merge gate
- [x] mix test green
- [x] LOC delta negative (production+test -13)
- [x] Auditor APPROVE

title: Replace Process.put smuggle with ExUnit.CaptureIO.with_io/3
